---
title: "1. Data Quality Assessment"
---

## Assessment approach

Before running any analysis, we systematically assessed the quality of each dataset along several dimensions. The table below summarizes the checks applied to each file.

| Check | Description |
|---|---|
| **File overview** | Row and column counts; identification of structurally empty columns |
| **Missing values** | Missing rate per variable, grouped by thematic block; flagging variables with meaningful missingness |
| **Duplicates** | Check for duplicate student or record identifiers |
| **Survey response quality** | Completion rate (`progress`) and response duration (`duration_min`) outliers |
| **Range validity** | Expected value ranges checked across thematic blocks: demographics, academic background, computer lab, hours & study habits, aspirations, social network, salary beliefs, and math assessment |
| **Derived variable consistency** | Cross-variable logical checks including scale average replication and reversal checks for reverse-coded items (e.g., grit, math enjoyment, math self-concept), as well as consistency between derived variables and their components (e.g., `educ_parent_max` vs. `educ_mother`/`educ_father`; math score totals vs. item sums) |

Additional dataset-specific checks are described within each section below. Thresholds were set on substantive grounds based on the study codebook and instrument documentation.

```{python}
import sys
import importlib
sys.path.insert(0, "../src")
import utils
import checks
importlib.reload(utils)
importlib.reload(checks)
from great_tables import GT, style, loc, md

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

utils.set_theme()

dfs = utils.load_all()
baseline  = dfs["baseline"]
schools   = dfs["schools"]
math_chat = dfs["math_chat"]
math_quiz = dfs["math_quiz"]
voc_chat  = dfs["voc_chat"]
voc_steps = dfs["voc_steps"]
```

---

## Data sources

The study draws on seven datasets from two sources: a Qualtrics baseline survey and interaction logs from the uDocz educational platform.

| Dataset | Grain | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `baseline_estudiantes` | Student | Pre-treatment survey covering demographics, psychological scales, math assessment, and post-secondary aspirations |
| `randomizacion_escuelas` | School | School-level treatment assignment and stratification variables used for randomization |
| `math_chat_casa` | Message | Student–AI math tutoring conversations conducted at home |
| `math_chat_escuela` | Message | Student–AI math tutoring conversations conducted at school, with topic and correctness data |
| `math_ponte_prueba` | Quiz attempt | Math quiz interactions from the platform, with correctness indicators |
| `voc_chat` | Message | Student–AI career guidance conversations across an 8-step vocational journey |
| `voc_steps` | Student × step | Student-level progress, time spent, and completion across the 8 vocational journey steps |

---

---

## Dataset assessments

Each dataset is assessed on a dedicated page. Detailed findings, tables, and recommendations are available within each report.

```{=html}
<table class="table table-bordered" style="table-layout:fixed; width:100%;">
  <colgroup>
    <col style="width:15%">
    <col style="width:40%">
    <col style="width:45%">
  </colgroup>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Dataset</th>
      <th>Key issues</th>
      <th>Recommendations</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Baseline student data</strong><br><a href="01a_baseline.html">View report →</a></td>
      <td>
        <ul style="margin:0; padding-left:1.2em;">
          <li>Math assessment correctness systematically miscoded (9/10 items, ~200 affected students each)</li>
          <li>Near zero internal consistency for all four main psychological scales (Grit, Locus of Control, Self-efficacy, and Metacognition)</li>
          <li>Several variables contain sentinel or out-of-range codes requiring recoding</li>
          <li>93 students flagged for low response quality</li>
        </ul>
      </td>
      <td>
        <ul style="margin:0; padding-left:1.2em;">
          <li>Verify scoring logic against de-anonymized data and original answer key; recode affected observations and recompute total score</li>
          <li>Check whether Qualtrics randomized response options; verify inter-item correlations in de-anonymized data before using scale averages</li>
          <li>Recode all sentinel and out-of-range values to missing as a mandatory preprocessing step</li>
          <li>Rerun main analyses excluding flagged students as a robustness check; verify gender coding against de-anonymized data</li>
        </ul>
      </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Schools randomization</strong></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Math chat logs</strong></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Math quiz</strong></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Vocational chat</strong></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><strong>Vocational steps</strong></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
      <td><em>Pending</em></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```
